notes: The original_text ("These values are preliminary and based on the best estimate of management, which is subject to change within the measurement period.") was confirmed verbatim in the J&J 10-K (jnj-20251228.htm, CIK 200406) but is entirely absent from the Pfizer 10-K (pfe-20251231.htm, CIK 78003). A grep of the full Pfizer filing found neither the target phrase nor even the token 'measurement period'. The test case notes acknowledge this mismatch ("actually J&J jnj-20251228") but the source_filing, source_url, and citation_as_written all point to Pfizer — making the stated source wrong. All other checks pass: the modified_text and claim are coherently paired, the citation appears verbatim in the report, the verdict (partially_supported) is correct for the overstated_confidence type, the type label accurately captures the preliminary-to-final reversal, and a subtlety rating of 4 is plausible.
suggested_correction: Change source_filing to "Johnson & Johnson", source_url to "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/200406/000020040626000016/jnj-20251228.htm", and citation_as_written to "Johnson & Johnson, Form 10-K for fiscal 2025 (jnj-20251228.htm), Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements — Acquisitions." The report text shown to the verifier should be updated to reflect the corrected company name and filing reference accordingly.
haiku_reason: The claim contradicts standard GAAP acquisition accounting principles. Under ASC 805, a "measurement period" (typically 12 months) exists precisely to allow adjustments to acquisition fair-value amounts as more information becomes available. Stating that amounts are "final" and "will not change during the measurement period" is self-evidently inconsistent with this accounting framework. Any legitimate 10-K disclosure would not make such an absolute assertion.
